title: analytics — field classification
description: Field-level classification per foundation's five-tier taxonomy. Analytics is behavioral; properties + context + attribution can carry near-PII (device fingerprints, IP hashes, session ids) so most substantive columns land in `confidential` or `internal`. NO raw PII (email, phone, name, address) is ever stored — enforced by PropertyValidator at capture time.

audit_log: audits.analytics.* rows produced by HasAuditable on AnalyticsEvent + AnalyticsProviderConfig + AnalyticsIdentity. Retained per foundation's audit_log tier.

notes: AnalyticsProviderConfig.config is RESTRICTED — contains API keys, project IDs, host URLs. AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest via Laravel Crypt. NEVER surfaced on any wire.

AnalyticsEvent.properties + context + attribution are CONFIDENTIAL — property values may include semi-identifying signal (feature ids, screen names) but NEVER raw PII (enforced at capture by PropertyValidator).
AnalyticsIdentity.device_fingerprint_hash is CONFIDENTIAL — hashed at browser boundary; NEVER raw fingerprint. Used only for anti-abuse.
AnalyticsDelivery.request_payload + response are CONFIDENTIAL — may include serialised property values. Hidden on tenant wire by default; reveal requires super_admin + ?include=raw_payload.
No `restricted` fields beyond the encrypted config column — analytics deliberately AVOIDS storing raw PII. Provider API keys are the only truly sensitive material.
